children.name: Leptosomus
children.description: A monotypic genus representing the sole member of an entire order (Leptosomiformes), making it one of the most evolutionarily distinct bird lineages. The Cuckoo-roller has a unique combination of morphological features that have long puzzled ornithologists — it resembles both cuckoos and rollers but is ancestral to neither. Its elusive nature and restricted island range add to its mystique.

children.name: Leptosomus discolor
children.description: The cuckoo-roller or courol is the only bird in the family Leptosomidae, which was previously often placed in the order Coraciiformes but is now placed in its own order Leptosomiformes. The cuckoo-roller is at the root of a group that contains the Trogoniformes, Bucerotiformes, Piciformes, and Coraciiformes. Despite its name, the cuckoo-roller does not share close evolutionary origins with cuckoos or rollers.
